page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 1488.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In multi-core benchmarks, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the middle 50%, though Geekbench single-core scores reach the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations scoring in the top 10%. However, PassMark memory scores sit in the middle 50%.

The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. This hardware acceleration is reflected in strong LLM inference speeds, particularly for medium (7B) and large (70B) models, which rank in the top 10% and top 25% respectively. Cryptographic capabilities are also robust, with PassMark CPU encryption tests scoring in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-192 offers high resource density for memory-intensive and parallelized compute workloads without the added cost of bundled GPUs or local NVMe storage. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 server configured with 192 vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating at 2.3 GHz across 96 physical cores, this instance features advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and PassMark memory scores align with mid-tier averages. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, representing a tradeoff that prioritizes high-density memory and compute resources. It is highly effective for memory-intensive workloads, including large database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1488 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 features 192 vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and Intel AMX acceleration for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
No local storage or GPU resources, optimizing cost for pure CPU and memory tasks
Suited for large database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
